title: CoT Prompting Patterns Cheat Sheet
concept: Consolidating step-back prompting, least-to-most prompting, and self-ask into a practical toolkit.

exercises.prompt: In the previous lesson, we saw that using Chain-of-Thought reasoning for simple tasks is disadvantageous because: why simple tasks can be counterproductive. What is a key downside of unnecessary downside of applying CoT to simple tasks?

options.text: It bypasses the LLM's safety guardrails completely.
It increases latency and API costs due to generating extra reasoning tokens.
It makes the final answer formatting strictly unpredictable.

exercises.explain: CoT adds token generation steps, which increases both the time to receive a response (latency) and the cost per API call.

cards.title: The Ultimate Reasoning Toolkit
cards.body: We have learned several ways to help LLMs reason. Now, let's organize these techniques into a handy cheat sheet so you know exactly which tool to deploy for any problem.

cards.title: Your CoT Prompting Toolkit
cards.body: A CoT toolkit helps you choose between patterns like Step-Back (shifting to a broader principle first), Least-to-Most (deconstructing hard tasks into smaller ones), and Self-Ask (having the AI ask and answer its own sub-questions).

cards.title: The Swiss Army Knife of Reason
cards.body: Think of these patterns like a Swiss Army knife. You wouldn't use a heavy saw to open a letter, nor a tiny blade to cut a branch; you pick the exact tool that fits the obstacle in front of you.

cards.title: Toolkit in Action
cards.body: When faced with a complex history question, a 'Self-Ask' prompt directs the AI to ask 'Who was the president in 1865?' and answer it before addressing the main query.

cards.title: More Patterns Isn't Always Better
cards.body: You don't need to use all CoT patterns at once. Mixing too many techniques can confuse the model, increase latency, and balloon your API costs unnecessarily.

cards.title: Matching Pattern to Problem
cards.body: Choose the right pattern based on the type of problem you need to solve.

left.text: Best for high-level rule abstraction and conceptual principles.

right.text: Best for sequential decomposition of mathematical or logical tasks.

exercises.prompt: Which prompting pattern is characterized by the model generating its own sub-questions to answer sequentially?
exercises.explain: Self-Ask prompting forces the model to explicitly generate intermediate questions and answers, mimicking a structured search dialogue.

exercises.prompt: Least-to-most prompting works best when a complex problem can be broken down into sequential, smaller sub-problems.
exercises.explain: Least-to-most prompting is ideal for problems that can be solved by tackling progressively harder sub-problems in a chain.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the sentence about abstraction.
exercises.sentence: When you want the AI to identify the underlying rule before solving a problem, use Step-Back Prompting.
exercises.explain: Step-Back Prompting works by abstraction, asking the AI to identify the core underlying concept before attempting the specific, complex question.

exercises.prompt: Identify the response that demonstrates the 'Self-Ask' prompting pattern.

options.text: To solve this, I will first look up the physics equation for gravity, then apply it to the mass of the object.
Are we looking for the founder of Company X? Yes. Who was that? It was Jane Doe. Now I can answer the main question.
Let's take a step back and define the general concept of thermodynamics before answering.

exercises.explain: Option B displays a structured self-questioning process, which is characteristic of the Self-Ask prompting pattern.

exercises.prompt: You are building an app that solves multi-step algebra word problems. Which prompting pattern is best suited for breaking down the math sequence step-by-step?
exercises.explain: Least-to-most prompting is specifically designed for tasks where the final answer requires combining multiple sub-steps in sequence, making it ideal for math word problems or multi-step logic.
